arts.ttl: Player says he wants to “make history” at Nottingham Forest – Not thinking about anything else
arts.sum: Nottingham Forest winger Jota Silva has insisted he wants to make history with the club and get into the Champions League. A Bola cover an interview with the winger today from an appearance on YouTube channel Entrelinhas with Afonso Figueiredo. arts.sum: Nottingham Forest will want to continue an impressive campaign with FA Cup progress at Exeter City this evening. arts.ttl: Exeter City FA Cup opponents revealed if they beat Nottingham Forest
arts.sum: Exeter City will be at home to Ipswich Town in the FA Cup fifth round if they cause a shock and beat Premier League Nottingham Forest tomorrow night. City have beaten Barnet, Chesterfield and Oxford United to reach round four for the first time since 1981.

arts.ttl: Exeter City hoping hectic end to transfer window pays off
arts.sum: “As disappointed as I’m sure supporters are that our top players are leaving, that is what we have to do and they have to then look at the next generation, or the next player that we bring in, to develop the team but also help the club financially in the future.” One or two suddenly became four as Tristan Crama’s loan spell was cut short by Brentford, who then sold him to Millwall, and as Crama packed his bags and returned to London, City were dealt a further blow by Johnly Yfeko picking up a second hamstring injury,, which looks set to rule him out for the rest of the season.
